title: [bug] the satellite role heuristic tests a MECHANISM (@dataclass), not the concept — pydantic models score as primary
description:
FIXED + MEASURED 2026-07-19. See CORRECTION at the bottom — the bead's headline claim was itself wrong.

THE FIX: _is_dataclass -> _is_data_container. A data container is identified by being DECLARED, not by
which library declares it: @dataclass (stdlib + pydantic.dataclasses), attrs @define/@frozen/@mutable, and
the BaseModel / NamedTuple / TypedDict bases. The *Cfg naming vocab stays rejected, and there is now a test
pinning that a class merely NAMED Config is still PRIMARY, so the rejection cannot quietly erode.

KNOWN GAP, recorded as a decision rather than an oversight: attrs' legacy @attr.s reduces to the trailing
name `s`, and admitting a bare `s` would match any @x.s -- a worse rule than the gap. Test pins it.

MEASURED, all three consumer repos, their real package lists from .copier-answers.yml:

  repo          classes(old -> new)   demeter   envy(old -> new)   composition
  cardiac-seg      23 -> 16              22        8 -> 1              0
  mindscape        30 -> 13               1       23 -> 0              0
  synthscape       19 -> 11               2        2 -> 2              0

ENVY IS THE REAL WIN and it validates the "one gap, three symptoms" thesis: envy excludes satellites as
targets, so fixing the role fixed envy almost entirely (33 findings -> 3 across all three repos). envy is
ENFORCED, so this is the part that would actually have broken consumers.

GRADUATION VERDICTS (the shape_contracts precedent):
  composition  0/0/0   -> blocks from day one, as planned
  envy         1/0/2   -> 3 findings total; enforceable, they are almost certainly true positives
  demeter      22/1/2  -> separate concern, as this bead always said. cardiac-seg is the outlier and has
                          the per-repo [tool.structure] demeter_max_depth escape. NOT a role problem.
  classes      16/13/11 -> ADVISORY. The survivors are largely GENUINE multi-abstraction files
                          (neuroscan/models/composite.py holds Backbone, Head and Model), i.e. consumer
                          refactoring work, not a scaffold defect.

CORRECTION to this bead's own premise: it projected "class-roles 23/30/14 would fail all three repos", but
`devtools.classes` is wired into ZERO runners -- noxfile, ci.yml and pre-commit all have zero references.
That gate cannot fire, so it could never have failed anyone. The column was measuring an engine no runner
invokes (the same absent-gate shape as iv5). The fix still matters, for a reason the bead did not give:
envy and archmap CONSUME the role classifier, and envy is enforced.

Wiring `classes` (advisory) belongs with iv5, which owns gate-set completeness.
Follow-up filed as dun.1: structural Protocol conformance, measured at ~8 of the 40 survivors.

title: roll v1.20.0 into the four consumers and triage what it finds
description:
`copier update` in cardiac-seg, mindscape, synthscape, mirage, then run each repo's gate set and record
what changed. checkpoint, not solo: the update itself is mechanical, but every finding is a judgment —
fix it, accept it, or decide the RULE is wrong. That last one is real and has happened (the satellite
heuristic was wrong, not the repos).

First thing to confirm, because it was broken until today: the pin actually resolves. `uv sync` fetching
`sdlc-devtools @ git+...@v1.20.0` is the end-to-end proof no test in the suite can give — the e2e
deliberately overrides it with a local source.

Expect, roughly: envy findings DROP (satellite fix), `typing.Any` appears advisory, pyrefly now aimed at
the floor each repo declares, and archmap graph.json regenerating with method-level call edges (a large
but mechanical diff — do not read it as drift).

Record per repo: what fired, what was fixed, what was accepted and why.

title: epic adoption: prove v1.20.0 in the consumer repos
description:
v1.20.0 is tagged and NOTHING has consumed it. Everything built since v1.19 — the satellite role fix, the
Any ban, pyrefly on the floor it promises, method-level call resolution, the 22-command gate set — has only
ever run against sdlc-devtools itself. The scaffold's whole thesis is that these gates hold on real repos;
until they run on real repos that is an untested claim.

Four consumers, not three: cardiac-seg, mindscape, synthscape, and mirage (mirage adopted v1.15.0 and
produced the acq audit, which is the best evidence so far that outside adoption finds what dogfooding
cannot).

Low sync cost is the premise, and it should be VERIFIED rather than assumed: nothing since v1.19.1 changes
a blocking gate's verdict except the satellite fix, which LOOSENS envy (33 -> 3 findings here). The Any ban
ships advisory. So a `copier update` should be quiet. If it is not, that is the finding.

This epic ends with: four repos on v1.20.0, their findings triaged, and a decision recorded on which
advisories graduate. It also validates the release path end to end — the pin was quietly broken all
session and only a real `copier update` proves it resolves.

title: epic GOAL: v1.20.0 release-ready — the scaffold passes its own bar
description:
GOAL. Everything the scaffold has built since v1.19.1 sits unreleased on dev: classes, composition,
contracts, envy. None of it reaches a consumer until v1.20.0 is TAGGED, and it must not be tagged while a
known-wrong heuristic would fail all three consumer repos. This goal ends with that tag.

The theme is DOGFOODING: the scaffold does not currently run on itself the gate set it sells. Every defect
found today came from the same shape -- a gate that does not run, a guard that probes something other than
what it guards, a claim asserted from memory instead of checked. So the goal is sequenced to make the tools
trustworthy FIRST, then to point them at ourselves, and only then to ship.

EXPLICITLY OUT OF SCOPE (next PR): the method tier and graph upgrades -- f1u (method-level call resolution
+ public/all depth split), 5cg (shared Resolver), a0a (self-edges). Also 1a8 (test_ prefix migration, needs
its own release for consumer coordination) and f9y (performance, measure-first).

PHASE 1 -- TRUST THE TOOLS (96j, y52)
Cheap, and everything after it depends on the result being believable.
  96j  has_node() probes shutil.which("npx") while subprocess needs npx.cmd, so 3 e2e tests are
       permanently red on Windows. Three tests you are trained to ignore is how a real jscpd regression
       walks in.
  y52  the same class of template/ruff-format bug shipped THREE times because the fast gate only catches
       lines that are too LONG, never lines ruff would JOIN. Render the templates and run ruff format
       --check in the unit suite: milliseconds instead of a 90s e2e round-trip, exact instead of heuristic.

PHASE 2 -- FIX WHAT BLOCKS THE TAG (az9, 166)
  az9  P0. The satellite heuristic tests a MECHANISM (@dataclass) rather than the concept (a declarative
       data container), so pydantic models score as primary -- and the template ships pydantic. Widen to
       BaseModel/attrs/NamedTuple/TypedDict, then RE-MEASURE all three repos. The *Cfg naming vocab is
       rejected; do not re-propose it.
  166  pyrefly missing-override-decorator vs requires-python >=3.11 (typing.override is 3.12+). A shipped
       gate that cannot be satisfied on our own stated floor.

PHASE 3 -- DOGFOOD (iv5, then 0y9)
  iv5  devtools runs a hand-rolled SUBSET: no pyrefly, vulture, jscpd, coverage, and no advisory explorers.
       Run the same gate set we ship. Expect real findings -- pyrefly strict over 17 engines is the big one.
  0y9  one of those findings, and the reason it went unnoticed: jscpd never ran here. 17x duplicated
       argparse plumbing over a half-formed interface (report() on 11, run_assert() on 6, --assert on 7).
       Define the interface FIRST, then the Cli is trivial and if-free. Ordered after iv5 so the gate finds
       it rather than us asserting it.

PHASE 4 -- THE NEW GATE (lph)
  lph  forbid Any: it opts out of the type system we made blocking. TID251 on the import is a complete ban
       (nested `dict[str, Any]` escapes every use-site rule but dies with the import). Two open questions
       to settle BEFORE it ships enforced: does pyrefly strict already reject a bare `dict` return (else
       this is half a rule and the dynamism just relocates), and what do the three consumer repos measure?

PHASE 5 -- RELEASE (ztw, then tag)
  ztw  the README front page still claims v1.2 / feature-complete.
  Then tag v1.20.0, and only then do consumers see any of it.

ACCEPTANCE
  - devtools runs the same gate set it ships, not a subset, with no self-exemption
  - every ENFORCED gate measured against cardiac-seg / mindscape / synthscape BEFORE the tag; anything
    still noisy ships advisory with a written graduation path (the shape_contracts precedent)
  - zero permanently-red tests in a local e2e run
  - v1.20.0 tagged, and the three consumers can copier update onto it without a wall of findings

PROCESS (standing, from this session): dev and main only -- no feature branches. No self-merging; the PR is
the gate. Verify claims by RUNNING them before writing them into a bead.
close_reason: v1.20.0 tagged at 2e71e6f — the acceptance condition. Released by release.yml on merge, not by hand, which also closes the process hole the goal kept tripping over.

title: calls terminate on the method, not the owning class (incl. intra-class)
description:
CallArrows currently aggregates per class, emitting (src_class, dst_class, kind, via). Both endpoints are
already derivable at walk time and are being thrown away:

- TARGET method — the attribute name in `self.repo.save()` IS `save`, in hand when the receiver resolves
- SOURCE method — the enclosing FunctionDef, currently collapsed by the per-class aggregation

RESOLUTION RULE (one rule, every case): the edge points at the class that DEFINES the method, found by
walking the project MRO from the declared receiver type.

- inherited from a project base -> lands on the base's method, where the code actually is
- declared type is a Protocol -> the Protocol defines it, so it lands there. This is not a special case; it
  is the call->interface partition falling out for free
- base is stdlib / third-party (dict.get, BaseModel.model_dump) -> the chain leaves the project, honest
  drop. We do not track outside our own packages, by decision
- dynamic (getattr, kwargs forwarding) -> unresolvable, honest drop (see the getattr gate task)

INTRA-CLASS calls are in scope: receiver is `self`, so the target class is the enclosing class and no
resolution is needed at all. A public method calling its own private helper is real internal structure and
is the reason the `all` depth stop exists. Note these are intra-FILE, so they roll up to a file self-loop
that the import graph legitimately drops — consistent with the roll-up invariant, not a violation of it.

Also sharpen the partition: `calls` -> method, `construct` -> class. Constructing is __init__, i.e. the
class as a whole, so behavioural coupling lands INSIDE the box and wiring lands ON it.

Unblocks the feature-envy method cut, which needs the same per-method resolution.

Removes the claim in archmap.py that method-level edges would be "inventing a precision the graph does not
have" — that comment is now wrong and must go with the change.
close_reason: CallArrows.edges() now returns CallEdge (both method endpoints); Resolver.definer() walks the project MRO to whoever DEFINES the method. construct split out as its own kind (lands on the class — constructing is __init__). class_edges() is the coarse projection, so contracts/graph/composition read what they always did. RESULT on devtools: method tier went 176 nodes / 0 edges -> 213 nodes / 240 method-to-method + 34 construct. Cross-class method edges 2 -> 76 after also resolving two receiver shapes the source states exactly: fluent construction (Trees(pkgs).walk()) and static calls via the class name (Resolver.field_types(...)) — those two were costing most of the connectivity.

title: depth control: split method into public / all stops
description:
DEPTHS becomes ['module', 'class', 'public', 'all'] in devtools/archviz/viewer.js. The scale stays ORDINAL
because public methods are a strict subset of all methods — no new widget, one more stop.

- `public` draws method nodes whose label has no leading underscore (87 of 176 in devtools)
- `all` draws every method node including private and dunder (+71 private, +18 dunder)
- readFilters() maps the stop to the node filter; the method node filter already requires its class present

The emitter needs no change — graph.json already carries every method node. This is viewer-side only.

Update the archmap schema tests: `test_the_method_tier_is_opt_in_in_the_viewer` asserts the DEPTHS literal,
so it must learn the 4-stop scale while still pinning that the view OPENS at `module`.
close_reason: DEPTHS is now ['module','class','public','all'] in viewer.js; readFilters maps the 4th stop to a privates filter and the method node filter drops leading-underscore labels at 'public'. devtools: 213 method nodes, 109 public / 104 non-public. Scale stays ordinal (public is a strict subset of all), so no new widget. test_the_method_tier_is_opt_in_in_the_viewer pins the literal + the module opening.

title: epic method tier: real method-level resolution + public/all depth split
description:
The method tier currently ships NODES ONLY (176 of them in devtools, zero edges among them). That makes it
a wall of labels rather than a graph: opening `method` depth adds detail but no connectivity.

Two objectives, tracked as children:

1. DEPTH SPLIT — `method` becomes two ordinal stops, `public` and `all`. Public methods are a subset of all
   methods, so the containment scale stays ordinal (module > class > public > all) and no new control is
   added. 176 methods split 87 public / 71 private / 18 dunder, so the public stop halves the wall while
   keeping the class's actual SURFACE readable.

2. METHOD-LEVEL BEHAVIOUR ARROWS — `calls` terminates on the method it calls, not on the owning class.
   Includes INTRA-CLASS calls (a public method calling its own private helper), which is where a class's
   internal structure actually lives and which the `all` stop exists to show.

Superseded during design: treating a private method as a method-tier "satellite" (muted, dropped by the
existing hide-satellites toggle). Rejected in favour of the depth split, which keeps the ordinal model and
keeps privates present as edge endpoints.
close_reason: All four children done. The method tier is now a graph rather than a wall of labels: 176 nodes / 0 edges -> 213 nodes / 274 edges, and the depth control gained the public/all split so the tier opens at a class's SURFACE before its internals.

title: GOAL: trustworthy architecture guardrails — typed class-relationship graph + viz
description: LONG GOAL (hand to /goal). Evolve graph.py from an import-only module graph into a typed class-relationship graph over one containment tree (package>file>class>method), arrows at their native level (import@file, inherits/holds@class, calls@method incl via=construct), metrics as edge-subset queries, plus the archmap rendering/diff. WHY: gates that talk about REAL dependencies (imports are noisy) so AI-authored architecture can be trusted because it is machine-enforced. DONE when, on the enriched e2e sample project: (1) containment tree + node roles emitted; (2) all four gated kinds carried as per-edge {kinds,weights}; (3) roll-up property holds (file-level roll-up == grimp import set cross-file; intra-file collapses to dropped self-loops); (4) EVERY new gate both runs CLEAN and is proven to BITE via assert_bites; (5) archmap renders kinds + filters + --diff changelog, graph.json deterministic; (6) every PRE-EXISTING gate stays green on the enriched seed. Spec + e2e acceptance matrix: docs/typed_class_graph_design.md. Children: sdlc-scaffold-4bl (arrows), sdlc-scaffold-433 (viz). Start at sdlc-scaffold-4bl.6 (A0 seed enrichment). NOT in scope: dead-import (ruff F401 + vulture own it).

description: PREREQ for the whole epic - the current seed (MathOps+Pipeline, one import edge) has no inheritance/composition/interfaces so it cannot exercise a single new arrow. Grow the e2e-owned seed to: types.py (Store Protocol PRIMARY + StoreConfig dataclass SATELLITE -> roles + intra-file pair), memory_store.py (MemoryStore(Store) -> inherits), repository.py (holds a Store field, calls it -> holds + call-to-INTERFACE), service.py (holds Repository, constructs MemoryStore -> via=construct to CONCRETE), keep math_ops/pipeline. title: A2 Batch1: structural arrow decomposition (inherits/holds/constructs/references) one AST pass
description: One AST pass emitting finer arrows at native level: inherits + holds (has-a, field-type annotations) @ class; references @ method (ungated attribute). calls is Batch2. Represent as DiGraph with per-edge {kinds:set,weights} (not MultiDiGraph). constructs folded into calls as via=construct subtag (Batch2). Self-check: cross-file structural arrows roll up to grimp imports; intra-file arrows = file self-loops with no import (expected). Kills import-noise; value without calls.
close_reason: Closed

title: A1 re-key graph nodes module->class + node roles (primary/satellite) + 2-primaries gate
description:
Nodes form a fractal containment tree: package>file>class>method ('everything is a module'). Class is the primary working level; tag roles primary/satellite; two PRIMARY classes in one file = a Structure gate. import graph = the file-level roll-up (self-loops dropped). Data goes to method level; the view folds to file (archmap containment already IS this tree).

ROLE HEURISTIC (constraint found while building the A0 fixture): a satellite is not only a config dataclass/enum/helper - it ALSO includes a same-file SUBCLASS of that file's primary. The A0 seed has errors.py (StoreError + KeyMissingError(StoreError)) and memory_store.py (MemoryStore + CapacityError(StoreError)); an exception hierarchy in one file is idiomatic and must NOT trip the 2-primaries gate. So: classify a class as satellite if it (a) is a dataclass/enum/config-ish, (b) subclasses another class defined in the SAME file, or (c) subclasses a base imported for that purpose (e.g. a module-local error family). Only genuinely independent peers count as primaries.

REUSE: graph.py already has _dotted (base-name resolution), _is_abstract, _module_file, abstractness (ClassDef walk) - the class enumeration builds on those, and lcom.py already collects per-method access sets.
close_reason: Closed

description: The interactive explorer: vendored cytoscape.js + expand-collapse + fcose (all MIT, inlined into one self-contained HTML), reads graph.json. Features: expand/collapse compound nodes (arbitrary depth), splittable meta-edges with counts (collapsedEdges), focus/neighborhood (highlightNeighbors), pan/zoom. No server, no Java. Publishable via Artifact or opened locally. Start from the expand-collapse repo demo.html (~hundreds of lines).

Verified: self-contained (0 external script src), wheel bundles all 6 assets, cardiac assembles (66KB json + 715KB html). 74 devtools tests, ruff/graph/ast-grep clean. Judge live on the real codebase: R1 arbitrary-depth collapse, R2 splittable meta-edge WITH count [7] (cytoscape's collapsedEdges), R3 focus/neighborhood highlight, R4 compound layout readable. DECIDE from what you see: (a) is the viewer good enough to build for real, (b) mermaid's fate — drop entirely vs keep one shallow inline figure. Throwaway output + decision, no shipped code.

Verified headless: collapsed view = one counted arrow per pair (cardioseg->core[137], cardioview->core[24], sums exact). title: archviz v2: interactive cytoscape architecture explorer (supersedes 2vt mermaid)
description: Pivot the archmap output from a committed static mermaid mirror-tree (shipped v1.5.0 / epic 2vt) to an INTERACTIVE explorer. Frontier decided across research (research/deep_dives/2026-07-16_interactive_archviz_libs.md): no tool does auto-derived + diffable + interactive at once (interactivity needs a runtime, a commit is static), so the pattern is: commit grimp's graph.json (the diffable erosion signal) + ship one self-contained cytoscape.js HTML viewer that hydrates it (expand/collapse arbitrary depth, splittable meta-edges with counts, focus/neighborhood, fcose compound layout) — no server (dodges tach --web remote upload), no Java (dodges Structurizr). Rip out the redundant deep mermaid mirror-tree + --check gate + pre-commit regen; keep AT MOST one shallow top-tier mermaid figure for a zero-dependency inline README glance (or drop entirely — a values call in .4). Edge types in scope: contains (nesting) + imports (deps). Inheritance/call-graph OUT (call graph is statically approximate; revisit only with full typing). Stays in sdlc-devtools; extract to a standalone python-autoarch only once real + a second consumer (not speculative).
close_reason: EPIC DONE — archviz v2 shipped v1.6.0. grimp -> committed graph.json (diff-truth) + self-contained interactive cytoscape viewer (manual aggregation: folded pair = one counted arrow; fold/expand any depth; focus-isolate). Vendored pinned libs in devtools/archviz/. Wired nox/CI/pre-commit + opt-in GitHub Pages (archviz_pages). Mermaid fully dropped. All 6 children closed. Spike->emitter->viewer->rip-mermaid->wiring->e2e+release.

title: archmap: auto-generated tiered architecture diagrams (autoviz)
description: Derive architecture from the package tree (folder = Python package = module, by normal import rules — no separate spec to author; reuse the 'packages' marking). grimp import graph -> aggregate edges per nesting tier with COUNTS -> emit TIERED, CLICKABLE mermaid to a docs/architecture/ mirror tree (one ARCHITECTURE.md per package level, click a box to drill into its subtree). Diffable in PRs: architecture erosion shows up as a diagram diff. DOC-GEN / advisory — import-linter stays the enforcement gate. Wire into the scaffold as a manual nox call + a pre-commit/CI stale-check hook so committed diagrams never drift from the real graph. Engine lives in sdlc-devtools (like graph.py); wiring + docs/architecture/ output live in the template. Edge counts double as coupling weight (same Ca/Ce grimp data graph.py already uses); a tier that renders as a hairball is itself a god-package smell.

Spike->engine->--check->wiring->e2e+docs. grimp combined graph -> tiered edge-counted clickable mermaid mirror-tree under docs/architecture/; doc-gen/advisory (import-linter stays the directional gate). Wired: manual nox -s archmap, pre-commit regen hook, CI advisory --check. All 5 children closed.

title: remove the ratchet: gates are legislated scaffold config, not freeze-current
description: The ratchet (auto-measure current metric + freeze it as ceiling + escape-hatch bump) is the wrong mechanism: seeding a ceiling from current state ratifies the mess (e.g. complexity=16 seeded from our worst function). Correct model = scaffold ships an OPINIONATED LEGISLATED default, tunable per-repo via config when needed (what copier config is for); knobs added on real need, not speculatively. Rip out: Ratchet primitive (_common) + tests; [tool.magic_literals]/[tool.complexity] ceilings; --max-* flags; freeze-current language. magic_literals -> ADVISORY (no universal number: 0 too strict, N arbitrary). complexity -> ADVISORY (ruff C901=10 stays THE fixed gate; radon just reports). Structure thresholds/vulture/coverage untouched (already legislated default+slot). v1.4.0.
close_reason: Ratchet removed: primitive + tests + [tool.magic_literals]/[tool.complexity] + --max-* flags gone. magic_literals + complexity now ADVISORY explorers (ruff C901=10 is the fixed complexity gate). Legislated-default+slot model kept for structure/vulture/coverage. Docs reframed decide-or-advisory. Suite 56p/2s, package green. v1.4.0 tagged+pushed.

title: v1.0 regressed lint_paths/jscpd_paths/data_env_var to when:false -> copier UPDATE cannot preserve non-default FACTS (adoption loss)
description:
Found adopting v1.0 into cardiac-seg (systole). v1.0 flipped three per-repo FACT vars to when:false (computed from packages): lint_paths, jscpd_paths, data_env_var. data_env_var was a when:true ANSWER in v0.17 (added by skr GAP3a precisely so a repo reading CARDIAC_DATA could override the CARDIOSEG_DATA default); v1.0 reverted it. lint_paths + jscpd_paths were also when:true answers in v0.17.

IMPACT on the UPDATE path (not fresh gen): copier update does NOT honor these for a consumer whose value differs from the packages-derived default. Empirically on cardiac (3 runs, v0.17->v1.0):
- copier update --defaults (existing .copier-answers.yml still holds lint_paths=core cardioseg cardioview tests, jscpd_paths=core cardioseg cardioview/web/src, data_env_var=CARDIAC_DATA): the rendered ci.yml recomputes from packages -> enforced lint scans only 'core cardioseg' (drops cardioview+tests), jscpd scans 'core cardioseg' (drops cardioview/web/src), tests env sets CARDIOSEG_DATA. The three keys are also STRIPPED from the regenerated .copier-answers.yml (when:false vars are not persisted).
- copier update --defaults --data lint_paths=... --data jscpd_paths=... --data data_env_var=CARDIAC_DATA: --data is IGNORED for when:false vars on the update path (same wrong render).
- CONTROL: a fresh copier COPY --defaults --data <same three> renders all three CORRECTLY (core cardioseg cardioview tests / cardioview/web/src / CARDIAC_DATA). So the override mechanism works on copy, only the UPDATE path drops it.

NET for a consumer: adopting v1.0 via copier update LOSES per-repo FACTS. For cardiac specifically: (a) enforced-lint scope shrinks to packages (cardioview + tests no longer linted), (b) jscpd DRY scope drops cardioview/web/src, (c) tests job sets CARDIOSEG_DATA but cardiac code reads CARDIAC_DATA -> data-gated CI tests ERROR (env unset) instead of skipping. (c) has NO LOCAL-SLOT workaround: the tests env: block is template-owned with no slot, and it precedes the base pytest step, so a consumer cannot inject CARDIAC_DATA. This blocks a no-loss v1.0 adoption for cardiac-seg (owner mandate: no losing stuff vs main).

RECOMMEND: restore lint_paths, jscpd_paths, data_env_var to when:true (asked, persisted in .copier-answers.yml) so their values survive copier update — reinstating skr GAP3a's design (data_env_var as an answer) and extending it to the two path vars. Keeping the packages-derived value as the DEFAULT is fine; the regression is making them non-overridable on update. Alternatively, if they must stay computed, honor an explicit .copier-answers.yml / --data override on the update path. VERIFY: a repo with lint_paths != packages does a copier update and the wider scope survives in the rendered ci.yml + persists in .copier-answers.yml.

Origin: cardiac-seg v1.0 adoption (bd cardiac-seg-zjaf / 44o4).
close_reason: FIXED v1.0.2. lint_paths/jscpd_paths/data_env_var made asked (when:true) so a non-default value persists in .copier-answers.yml + survives copier update (a when:false computed var is neither stored nor honored on the update path). data_env_var gated when=enable_ml and MOVED below enable_ml in copier.yml — copier evaluates vars top-to-bottom so a when-expr only sees vars above it; that ordering was why it wasn't persisting. VERIFIED: new e2e test_copier_update_preserves_nondefault_fact_vars generates with lint_paths='core viewer tests' + data_env_var=CUSTOM_DATA, runs copier update, asserts both survive in the rendered ci.yml AND persist in .copier-answers.yml (+ the portable rule still flows in). 96 tests green. NOTE for consumers: cardiac's current .copier-answers.yml holds only packages (the FACTS were already stripped by the v1.0 update) — cardiac must re-set lint_paths/data_env_var once on its next update via --data or the answers file; thereafter they persist.

Scaffold dropped it in the de-opinionate refactor arguing contracts are project-specific + graph.py covers cycles. But graph.py _cycles catches only bidirectional cycles, NOT one-way forbidden imports (core->trainer no-cycle passes graph, fails import-linter). The GATE is universal; only the CONTRACTS vary. Fix: enable_import_linter bool (default true); ships [tool.importlinter] with root_packages=packages + a kernel-independence starter contract (packages[0] forbids importing packages[1:]) in a LOCAL-SLOT, ONLY when >1 package (single-package = no layers). title: epic the shipped gate runner: one process, not twelve
description:
Measured: ~0.32s process + 0.19s import + 0.15s work per gate, so a ~12-gate run spends ~6s starting
Python and ~1.3s analysing. Startup dominates by 4-5x, on every commit, in every consumer repo.

The thing changed: HOW the gates are invoked. One runner that imports the engines and runs them in-process,
replacing twelve `python -m devtools.X` subprocesses. Newly feasible because 0y9 unified the engine
interface (report() -> str, run_assert() -> int) — before that there was nothing uniform to drive.

Ends with: the template's noxfile and pre-commit driving the runner, and a measured before/after on a real
tree. This is what makes f9y.4 (Rust) unnecessary — it attacks the 1.3s, not the 6s.
close_reason: Its one child shipped: the analyzers run in one process, 1977ms -> 670ms on the devtools tree, wired into both the package's noxfile and the template's.

title: gate type PLACEMENT: a type declared in A but produced only in B
description:
From acq observation 1. CLAUDE.md states the rule — a supplementary type lives BESIDE its producer, never
bucketed by KIND (`*_types.py`, `schema.py`) — and no axis expresses it, so mirage accumulated two bucket
modules that passed every gate: well-formed, not dead, not cyclic, and NOT god-modules (fan-in high but
fan-out ~0, so that rule's AND never fires). Visible only to a human reading the graph.

The signal is precise and buildable from what already exists (arrows.py / calls.py / classes.py):
  "type T is declared in module A but constructed/returned only in module B" -> single producer, misplaced.
  Multiple producers = genuinely shared vocabulary, belongs at the package root, SKIP.

A weaker bucket smell as a second rule: N types, zero behaviour, imported by K modules of its own package,
not __init__, not a dispatch table.

CARVE-OUTS that must exist or it over-fires (both stated in acq, both real): shared vocabulary consumed by
several producers, and declaration-only CLI dispatch tables.

Evidence it is worth doing: dissolving both buckets in mirage was a NET LINE REDUCTION and surfaced design
facts they had hidden — a duplicate narrow view of a payload (twice), and a placement that resolved by
import direction because the reverse was a hard cycle.

Advisory first, measured on all four consumer repos before it blocks.

title: epic the rules we state but never gate
description:
The project's own line: "an absent gate is the thing this project treats as a bug, because a gate that
does not run is indistinguishable from a clean codebase." This epic points that at the rules we WRITE DOWN
and never check. Every instance found so far followed the same arc — stated in prose, believed, violated
quietly, then discovered by a human reading code.

Already burned us, twice, in one session:
  - copier.yml stated "devtools_ref must track the tag the matching sdlc-devtools was published at".
    Nothing enforced it; it shipped pointing at a tag that did not exist. (fixed, bd 540)
  - CLAUDE.md states a supplementary type lives BESIDE its producer and never in a kind-bucket. Nothing
    enforces it; mirage accumulated schema.py — 200 lines, 19 symbols, zero `def`, serving five producers.

Still open, and the substance of this epic: type PLACEMENT has no axis at all, a test that cannot fail
passes every gate we own, and getattr defeats every static guarantee we sell downstream.

Sequencing note: each of these starts ADVISORY and graduates once a real tree reaches zero — the
shape_contracts / pyrefly-strict precedent. Do not ship a new blocking gate against unmeasured repos.

title: nothing checks the shipped devtools_ref pin resolves
description:
`copier.yml` ships `devtools_ref` (default "v1.20.0") into every generated pyproject as
`sdlc-devtools @ git+...@{{ devtools_ref }}`. That value is the ONLY thing connecting a consumer to an
analyzer release, and nothing verifies it.

Two holes, and they compound:
  1. no test asserts devtools_ref matches sdlc-devtools' own `version` in pyproject.toml
  2. no test asserts the tag it names actually EXISTS on the remote

The README-vs-package version match IS tested (test_the_readme_headline_version_matches_the_package), so
the drift that was caught is the cosmetic one, while the drift that breaks `uv sync` is not.

Why the existing suite structurally cannot catch it: the e2e injects a `[tool.uv.sources]` override so
`uv sync --extra devtools` builds the LOCAL package instead of fetching the ref (conftest ~line 164). That
is right for e2e — you want the code under development, not a published tag — but it means the pin is
never once resolved by anything we run.

Found live: devtools_ref was already "v1.20.0" while no v1.20.0 tag existed, so any consumer running
`copier update` would have taken a pyproject whose devtools extra cannot resolve. Harmless only because
nobody updated in the window.

Fix: a unit test comparing copier.yml's devtools_ref to the package version (cheap, catches hole 1 and is
the one that would have fired here). Hole 2 wants a network check, so it belongs in release CI rather than
the unit suite — or make tagging the thing that SETS devtools_ref, so they cannot disagree.
close_reason:
Closed by the work it described. Two halves shipped in PR #25:

  - release.yml derives the tag from sdlc-devtools/pyproject.toml on merge to main, so 'the tag exists' is a consequence of merging rather than a remembered step. Verified live: merging #25 published v1.20.0 at 2e71e6f on the workflow's first real run.
  - a unit test holds copier.yml's devtools_ref to the package version, turning copier.yml's stated-but-unenforced rule into a red test. Verified by drifting the pin to v1.19.0 and watching it fail, rather than trusting it would.

One trap found on the way: a tag pushed with GITHUB_TOKEN does NOT fire 'push: tags', so automating the tag would have silently stopped api-diff — the release's own breaking-change gate — from ever running again. api-diff gained workflow_dispatch and release.yml invokes it explicitly; confirmed firing on the v1.20.0 release.

Also moved api-diff onto pull_request: it was advisory AND ran after the tag, so its promise to make the releaser 'set the version accordingly' was unachievable — the version was already published. It now speaks while major-vs-minor is still open.

title: no machine check on the viewer — every layout change was judged by eye
description:
The sharpest gap in the epic. Across a whole session of layout work the ONLY verification was `node --check`
(syntax) plus the user looking at a screenshot. Nothing would catch a regression, and one bug shipped and
was caught by eye that a check would have caught for free: seeding new siblings within +/-30px of their
parent, which made the deep stops unreadable.

Also caught by eye rather than by tooling, same session:
  - try/catch wrapped layout CONSTRUCTION, not run() — fcose throws on run, so the cose fallback was dead
  - a call to a class's own `self.<field>()` reported as a resolver miss

Not asking for pixel-diffing. Cheap invariants that would have caught real bugs here:
  - no two sibling compound boxes overlap by more than N% after layout settles
  - no node sits outside its parent's box
  - a depth change leaves every surviving node within X of where it was (the incremental promise)

Needs a headless DOM. cytoscape runs under jsdom, and the viewer is one self-contained file, so a node test
harness is plausible without a browser. Verify that first — cytoscape's renderer may need a canvas stub.

title: run the gate set in ONE process instead of twelve
description:
The only lever here a CONSUMER feels, and the one that makes the Rust child unnecessary.

MEASURED on cardiac-seg (102 files, our largest consumer):
  per gate:  ~0.32s process start + ~0.19s import devtools + ~0.15s actual analysis
  all eight analyzers together: ~1.3s of compute
  a lint run fires ~12 of these, so ~6s of startup against ~1.3s of work

Analysis is roughly a QUARTER of a gate's cost. Startup is the rest, and it is paid per gate because each
one is `uv run python -m devtools.<tool>` in its own process.

FIX: one process that constructs the engines once and runs them in sequence — one interpreter, one
`import devtools`, and (with 5cg) one AST walk shared across engines instead of each building its own
Resolver. The Cli from 0y9 already gives every engine a uniform report()/run_assert(), so a runner can
iterate engines without knowing which is which.

ORDERING: 5cg (shared Resolver) is the compute half of this and should land WITH it, but both want f1u.2
first — that rewrites CallArrows and adds an MRO walk, so doing either now means optimising an API that is
about to change and measuring a cost profile that is about to change.

CONSTRAINT: `python -m devtools.<tool> [--assert]` stays. It is documented, wired into every template
runner, and pinned in consumers. A batch runner is an ADDITION for the runners to call, not a replacement.
close_reason:
devtools/run.py ships: one interpreter, one import, one parse of the tree shared by every engine that
resolves names. MEASURED on the devtools tree: 1977ms -> 670ms, 13 processes -> 1.

CORRECTION to this bead's own numbers: it estimated ~510ms of startup per gate (~6s per run) from
cardiac-seg. Measured on devtools it is ~155ms per gate, so the real saving is ~1.3s, not ~5s. The
direction holds and the argument against f9y.4 is unchanged (Rust attacks the compute, not the startup),
but the magnitude was overstated and should not be requoted from here.

Kept the constraint the bead set: `python -m devtools.<tool> [--assert]` is untouched. The runner is an
addition for runners to call.

MECHANISM, NOT POLICY — explicit --gate/--report lists rather than inferring from whether an engine owns a
run_assert, so `classes` stays advisory and `shape_contracts` stays ML-only without the runner knowing.
Also: every engine runs even after one fails, so a commit breaking three gates is reported once.

Wired into the devtools noxfile and the TEMPLATE noxfile. Deliberately NOT into CI or pre-commit: a named
CI step says which gate failed, and a pre-commit id can be skipped individually — batching would trade that
identity for a saving those runners feel less.

The scaffold's own gates caught three real defects in it (two Protocols as peers when a Gate extends an
Engine; Batch.report shadowing the engine verb; two top-level helpers). The gate-wiring guard was taught
the `--gate a,b,c` form rather than relaxed.

title: graduate devtools pyrefly to BLOCKING: 9 errors, 7 of them one config-boundary pattern
description:
DONE 2026-07-19. pyrefly strict on devtools 52 -> 0, success_codes swallow removed, gate BLOCKING. Proved
it bites with a bare `-> list` (the unnamed-Any case TID251 cannot see and strict must catch).

The 9 resolved as predicted — 7 were one config-boundary pattern, fixed with four narrowing helpers on
Pyproject (table / str_list / str_of / rows) so `object` becomes typed in ONE place. The other two: radon's
missing stubs went to ignore-missing-imports as a declared fact, and magic_literals' bad-return dissolved
once _is_token became a TypeGuard[str] — the same shape as is_self_attr.

UNCOVERED A REAL PRE-EXISTING GAP while doing it: devtools had no [tool.ruff] section, so `ruff format`
resolved ruff's DEFAULT 88 columns against a 120-column house style, and its select omitted E501 so nothing
could notice. Wiring `ruff format --check` in iv5 without checking the resolved width was worse than the
absent gate it replaced — it would have enforced 88 and produced churn that reads as compliance. I hit
exactly that: ~690 lines of reformat across 20 files, including files I never touched.

Recovery is worth recording because it is not obvious: ruff formatting is NOT reversible. Splitting at 88
inserts MAGIC TRAILING COMMAS, which pin the exploded form at any width, so re-running at 120 does nothing.
It needs format.skip-magic-trailing-comma to collapse them — and that over-corrects on code that was
deliberately exploded, so untouched files had to be reverted individually.

line-length = 120 and E501 now exist in sdlc-devtools, so this cannot recur silently.

title: role: recognise a structural implementation of a same-file Protocol as a satellite
description:
IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-19 — correct in principle, and MUCH smaller in effect than this bead estimated.

THE RULE: a class whose PUBLIC method set is a superset of a same-file Protocol's is a structural
implementation of it, hence a local specialisation, hence a satellite. Precise but incomplete, matching the
resolver's rule — a partial implementer stays PRIMARY, so nothing is mislabelled. Two guards that are not
obvious: a Protocol never implements anything (it would satisfy its own methods and label itself a satellite
of itself), and an EMPTY Protocol matches nothing (every class satisfies a contract with no methods).

MEASURED IMPACT, and my estimate was 8x optimistic:

  repo          multi-primary before -> after
  cardiac-seg        16 -> 16   (0)
  mindscape          13 -> 12   (-1)
  synthscape         11 -> 11   (0)

This bead predicted ~8 of 40. The 8 was a PROXY — I counted files CONTAINING a same-file Protocol, which is
not the same as files where the other classes fully implement it. Same measure-by-proxy error as the lph
grep that read 215 where the real rule found 49.

WHY SO SMALL, and this is the useful part: the rule works exactly as designed on the motivating case.
mindscape core/data/fnirs/clean.py went 5 primaries -> 2:

  primary   Cleaner    <- the Protocol, i.e. the contract
  satellite Cbsi       <- structural implementations, now recognised
  satellite Detrend
  satellite Chain
  primary   Clean      <- the subject

But the FILE is still flagged, because 2 primaries still exceeds 1. So the change reduces primaries-per-file
without usually dropping below the threshold: the Protocol AND its facade both stay primary.

FOLLOW-ON QUESTION FOR THE `classes` GATE, which this measurement exposes: the dominant surviving pattern is
"a Protocol plus its factory/facade in one file". That is arguably ONE cohesive subject — a contract and the
thing that builds implementations of it — rather than two competing subjects. If that reading holds, it is
the next real reduction, and it is a rule question rather than a classifier bug. Not filed as work yet
because it needs a decision on the concept, not a measurement.

title: forbid Any in type hints (ban the import — the annotation that opts out of annotation)
description:
IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-19, ADVISORY for consumers and ENFORCED for devtools. The difference is evidence, not a
carve-out in either direction.

MEASURED FIRST (the az9 lesson), by running the real rule rather than grepping:

  cardiac-seg   49 TID251 violations
  mindscape     47
  synthscape     0
  sdlc-devtools  0

My initial grep said 215 / 184 / 0 — nearly 4x the real count, because `\bAny\b` also matches prose and
partial patterns. Running the actual gate is the only measurement that means anything.

WHY ADVISORY: ~75% of the violations are `dict[str, Any]` at JSON/config boundaries (86 of ~114 in
mindscape). That is a refactor into a TypedDict/Protocol per boundary, not a rename — real work in the
consumer repos, and exactly the situation the graduation rule exists for.

MECHANISM: TID251 rides `ruff_advisory_select`, which was empty and whose own comment already described this
case — "a seam: a future code that needs whole-tree visibility before it's honest to gate rides here". It
graduates into `ruff_select` when a real tree reaches 0. The ban itself is
[tool.ruff.lint.flake8-tidy-imports.banned-api] on `typing.Any`, in both the template and devtools.

THE RULE IS COMPLETE, which was this bead's open question and is now settled:
  TID251 closes the NAMED Any, including `dict[str, Any]` — which no use-site rule sees (not even ANN401),
    yet cannot exist once the name cannot be imported
  pyrefly strict closes the UNNAMED one — bare generics, empty containers, unannotated params, via the
    implicit-any family (40 of the 52 errors iv5 found on devtools were exactly these)
So the escape hatch this bead worried about — "banning Any pushes people to a bare dict, and nothing catches
that" — was already closed by a gate we ship. It just was not running on us.

devtools takes it ENFORCED because it measures 0: the package selling the rule should be the one repo that
cannot regress on it.

title: gate getattr (dynamic attribute access defeats every static guarantee we ship)
description:
getattr / setattr / hasattr with a non-literal name defeats the whole static story: the type checker cannot
check it, the arch graph cannot resolve it, and vulture cannot see the attribute is used. Every guarantee
the scaffold sells is void on those lines, silently.

Owner's call: it should not exist in our code at all.

Options to evaluate (cheapest first):
  - ruff has no direct rule, but flake8-bugbear B009/B010 (getattr/setattr with a CONSTANT literal) is
    already reachable via ruff's B ruleset — that catches the trivially-rewritable cases only
  - the NON-literal case (the one that actually breaks resolution) needs an ast-grep rule, which the
    scaffold already ships and wires
  - decide the escape hatch: a per-repo allowlist in [tool.structure], or a noqa-style inline marker

Discovered while designing method-level call resolution (f1u.2), where getattr is one of the two honest
unresolvable cases — the other being stdlib/third-party, which is out of scope by decision, not by defeat.

title: a resolver miss is a FINDING, not a silent fallback (cross-check vs pyrefly)
description:
Once calls resolve to a method, a resolved receiver whose method cannot be found is not imprecision to
hedge against — it is a BUG, and it is mechanically detectable. Verified 2026-07-19 with pyrefly 0.15.0:

  probe.py:18:9-25: Object of class `Repo` has no attribute `delete` [missing-attribute]
  (same run silently ACCEPTED an inherited Child.flush() from Base — so pyrefly's resolution is exactly
   the MRO walk f1u.2 implements)

So under a pyrefly-clean repo, a miss can only mean one of:
  a) our MRO walk has a gap        -> our bug, fix the resolver
  b) the chain left the project    -> expected, drop quietly (stdlib / third-party base)
  c) pyrefly is not green here     -> the repo has a real bug pyrefly would have caught

That makes archmap a CROSS-CHECK on the type checker, free, on every consumer repo.

Scope: log a miss loudly (case a/c distinguishable by whether every base in the chain is in-project). Do
NOT gate yet — pyrefly landed in v1.19.0 and synthscape is only now picking it up, so the clean-repo
premise is not yet true everywhere. Gate only once the three consumer repos are pyrefly-green.
close_reason:
Miss channel shipped, reported not gated (as scoped — pyrefly is still landing on synthscape, so the clean-repo premise is not true everywhere yet). Resolver.leaves_project() distinguishes the two cases the bead named: every ancestor in-project = a real finding; a chain reaching a stdlib/third-party base = an expected quiet drop. CallArrows.misses surfaces in report().

IT PAID FOR ITSELF ON FIRST RUN: exactly one miss on our own tree, Cli.run -> Cli.engine — and it was OUR bug, not the repo's. 'self.<field>()' invokes the object held in that field; asking which class defines a method named 'engine' is the wrong question. Fixed + regression test. devtools is now at 0 misses, i.e. the MRO walk resolves every in-project call it attempts.

title: README front page still claims v1.2 / feature-complete (now 1.20.0 with ~10 new gates)
description:
DONE 2026-07-19. The headline now leads with what changed since 1.2 (the guardrails reaching BELOW the
import graph via typed class arrows) instead of a superseded "feature-complete" claim.

Also fixed a self-contradiction the bead did not mention: the properties section called the analyzers
"shipped as source under devtools/" three sections above the part that correctly describes them as a pinned
package.

"Fully self-gating" needed care, not a version bump. After iv5 it is much closer to true, but pyrefly and
class-roles are advisory on devtools, so "fully" would overstate it. The wording now says which gates run,
which two are advisory, why, and what makes them block.

GUARDED: test_the_readme_headline_version_matches_the_package compares README **vX.Y** against
sdlc-devtools' version at major.minor. Proved to bite. Nothing watched that number before, which is exactly
how it drifted eighteen versions.

VERIFIED rather than assumed: pages.yml writes a root index.html redirecting to /architecture/ and the
remote is dimiturtrz/sdlc-scaffold, so the viewer link resolves. Caveat recorded in the commit — the STABLE
Pages view builds from main, so it shows the pre-arrow graph until v1.20 merges (dev is at
/architecture/preview/).

title: unit-gate the python TEMPLATES against ruff format, not just line length
description:
DONE. tests/unit/test_scaffold_consistency.py now RENDERS template/*.py.jinja with jinja2 across the full
2^3 switch matrix (enable_ml x use_import_linter x ruff_advisory_select) and checks the output with the
PINNED ruff -- both `format --check` and `check --select E501`.

Both directions are covered, which is the whole point: the old heuristic compared jinja-FREE lines against
120 and could only see a line that was too LONG. It was structurally blind to the opposite -- a call wrapped
across lines that ruff would JOIN because the single-line form fits, which is the shape that shipped three
times (pyrefly, demeter, envy).

PROVED IT BITES: injecting exactly that shape (unwrapping --assert from the demeter step so the joined form
fits) fails the gate on all 8 variants with "Would reformat", while E501 reports "All checks passed!". Two
rules, two directions, neither redundant. Restore was done from an in-memory backup with try/finally, NOT
`git checkout` -- that command destroyed uncommitted work in this same file earlier this session.

FOUND A REAL BUG ON ITS FIRST RUN: the lint() docstring rendered to 125 chars whenever use_import_linter is
on. Invisible to the old test (the line contains {%), and invisible to the e2e for TWO independent reasons
-- no e2e combo sets use_import_linter, and the enforced ruff check runs over lint_paths (package dirs), so
a generated noxfile.py is only ever seen by the advisory whole-tree run. Shortened the docstring.

The gate is deliberately STRICTER than what consumers enforce on themselves: these are files we author and
ship, so a violation that would merely be advisory noise in their repo is a defect in ours.

CORRECTION to this bead's original text: it claimed "copier/jinja2 is already a dev dependency via the e2e,
so no new tooling". False -- the e2e shells out to `uvx copier`, an isolated tool env, and jinja2 was not
importable. Added jinja2>=3 to the dev group.

Also: only ONE python template exists (template/noxfile.py.jinja), so the feared per-branch-combination
cost was 8 renders and a single ruff invocation, not the fiddly job the bead anticipated.

title: devtools self-gates only a SUBSET of the gate set it ships (pyrefly/vulture/jscpd/coverage absent)
description:
WIRED 2026-07-19. The package now runs the gate set it ships: 12 commands -> 22, plus a new cov session.

  ruff check              enforced (was already there)
  ruff format --check     ADVISORY, matching the template. Absent before, which is why four files had
                          drifted out of format with nothing to notice
  vulture 80 / 60         ENFORCED / advisory — measured 0 findings at both, so it blocks from day one
  pyrefly strict          ADVISORY with a graduation path — 52 -> 9 errors; the success_codes swallow comes
                          off when it reaches 0
  jscpd                   ENFORCED — 0.00% on python. Needs --ignore '**/archviz/**' because the vendored
                          cytoscape/fcose bundles are third-party; passed on the CLI, not added to
                          devtools/jscpd.json, since that config SHIPS to consumers and this path is ours
  explorers               lcom, data_clumps, state_candidates, arrows, calls all added (advisory)
  classes                 ADVISORY everywhere — 16/13/11 on the consumer repos even after az9, and those
                          survivors are genuine multi-abstraction files, not a classifier bug
  cov (new session)       floor 80 = the TEMPLATE'S OWN DEFAULT, not a number fitted to what devtools
                          scores. Measured 83%, so it blocks with real headroom. Advisory 95% target too

`uvx nox` -> lint 11s, test 3s, cov 2s, all green.

ON ADVISORY-VS-ABSENT, since this bead is about self-exemption: a wired-but-advisory gate is a RATCHET with
the work visible and counted. An ABSENT gate is indistinguishable from a clean codebase — that is the whole
failure this bead names, and it is not the same thing. pyrefly and classes are advisory HERE for the same
measured reasons they would be advisory anywhere, not because it is us.

EVIDENCE THE WIRING MATTERS, from this session alone: ast-grep (already wired) caught a top-level function I
added — in the package whose own rule forbids it, while this bead was open. archmap --check (already wired)
caught the stale graph.json that moving that helper onto its class produced. And ruff format (NOT wired)
caught nothing, which is exactly why four files had drifted.

Remaining, tracked and not blocking: 9 pyrefly errors, 7 of them one pattern — `object` out of
Pyproject.tool_section() used without narrowing (contracts 5, omit, shape_contracts). They want what
[tool.structure] got: a typed reader per section, narrowing once at the config boundary. The other two are
radon's missing stubs (a pyrefly ignore-imports entry) and a real bad-return in magic_literals.

title: devtools CLI: one home for engine plumbing (17x duplicated argparse + a half-formed engine interface)
description:
DONE 2026-07-19, in two steps: the interface first, then the Cli on top of it.

RESULT: main() bodies 284 -> 147 lines; 13 of 17 engines DECLARE their CLI instead of implementing it.
analytics, archmap and config keep their own parsers — genuinely different verbs, named as bespoke rather
than quietly skipped.

STEP 1 found the interface was MORE fragmented than this bead recorded — three report shapes, not two:
instance report(self) on 7, static report(rows) on 4, static report(artifact, opts) on 3. The third only
surfaced because the uniformity TEST went in before I assumed I had found them all; it failed on graph,
complexity and magic_literals. Building the Cli on my two-shape survey would have broken on exactly those.

STEP 2's design turns on one thing worth recording: argparse's add_argument is OVERLOADED (`type=` is
rejected alongside store_false), so a single kwargs dict for every flag shape can only be typed
dict[str, object] — which argparse refuses — or Any, which lph had just banned here. Splitting into Flag (a
value option) and Switch (a boolean off-switch), each calling add_argument with its own correctly-typed
arguments, made the parser if-free AND checkable. Polymorphism where I had reached for a dict.

THREE OF OUR OWN GATES CAUGHT THIS CODE AS IT WAS WRITTEN, which is the strongest evidence in this whole
goal that iv5 and dun.2 paid for themselves:
  demeter    flagged self.engine.__module__.rsplit as 3-deep
  graph      flagged cli.py for no mirror test AND 2 primary classes
  pyrefly    flagged 12 errors, including bare dicts in accepted() — the exact thing lph had just banned

The 2-primary finding was dun.1's own pattern landing on my file: an `Engine` Protocol beside `Cli`. The fix
was NOT to split — the Protocol was decorative. Nothing annotated against it and test_engine_interface.py
already enforces that contract package-wide, so it was documentation wearing a type's clothes.

TWO BEHAVIOURAL BUGS the tests caught, both invisible to review:
  prog/logger must resolve via inspect.getfile, not __module__ — under `python -m devtools.x` the module
    reloads as `__main__`, so --help advertised `python -m devtools.__main__` and every log line was
    labelled __main__ instead of the tool that emitted it
  an unpassed flag arrives as None and OVERRODE each engine's own default, crashing data_clumps with None
    reaching range()

The `ap` -> `parser` rename folded in: the shared parser is built once, in Cli.parser().

NOT DONE, and it should not be: this bead's original goal of "no engine keeps a top-level main()". That
rested on the self-exemption claim already refuted in the correction — py-top-level-function exempts `main`
IN THE RULE, for consumers too. main() also remains how the interface test discovers engines. Each is now
two lines instead of fifteen, which was the real win.

title: feature-envy gate (needs METHOD-level call edges)
description: Split out of A4 (4bl.4). Feature-envy = a METHOD that touches another class's members more than its own. A3's CallArrows aggregates edges per CLASS, so this needs a method-level cut (per-method own-vs-foreign access counts) plus a resolution-confidence guard so the ratio is only judged where most receivers resolved. Also needs an exclusion vocab for legitimate delegation (mappers/serializers/visitors/facades read another class heavily BY DESIGN) - same pattern lcom uses for ABC/stub. Kept separate rather than half-built into A4, whose other two gates (composition cycles, forbidden-use contracts) are sound at class granularity.
close_reason: Closed

title: pyrefly missing-override-decorator conflicts with requires-python >=3.11 (typing.override is 3.12+)
description:
FIXED 2026-07-19 — and the bead had it BACKWARDS. Probed with the PINNED pyrefly (1.1.1, not the 0.15.0 I
first reached for) on `from typing import override` under a Base/Child override:

  explicit python-version = "3.11"                  1 error   <- the described conflict, reproduced
  explicit python-version = "3.12"                  0 errors
  NO key, requires-python = ">=3.11"                0 errors  <- what the template actually shipped

So the premise "[tool.pyrefly] pins python-version to the 3.11 floor" was false: there was no such key, and
pyrefly does NOT derive its target from requires-python. The conflict could not occur.

THE REAL DEFECT IS THE INVERSE, and worse. Unset, pyrefly checks against its own default (>=3.12), so a
consumer declaring >=3.11 got a GREEN type check for code that ImportErrors at runtime on 3.11. A checker
aimed at a NEWER interpreter than you ship is worse than no checker there, because it reports confidence it
does not have. None of the three listed options (raise the floor / disable the rule / document
typing_extensions) addressed that, because all three assumed the gate was already targeting the floor.

FIX, both halves, in order:
  1. add `python-version` to [tool.pyrefly] so the checker targets the floor the project promises
  2. raise the floor to >=3.12, which makes typing.override legal and the override rule satisfiable

Raising the floor is safe and was verified, not assumed: synthscape ALREADY requires >=3.12,<3.13 (isaacsim
6.0 ships cp312 only), and cardiac-seg / mindscape had merely inherited the template's >=3.11 default. No
consumer needed 3.11. Option (b) was rejected because disabling missing-override-decorator weakens the type
story we are simultaneously strengthening in lph; option (c) adds a runtime dep to every consumer for one
decorator.

GUARDED: test_the_type_checker_targets_the_python_floor_the_project_promises asserts requires-python and
[tool.pyrefly] python-version name the same version. Proved it bites (drift the target to 3.13 -> exit 1).
The two values were previously free to disagree with nothing noticing, which is how this shipped.

title: B5 archmap --diff changelog view + PR surfacing (trust centerpiece)
description: archmap --diff <ref>: added (green) / removed (red) edges at class level from two graph.json snapshots. Surface on PR ('this change added call edge domain->infra'). The trust feature: gate enforces, diff explains how a change moved the architecture in real-dependency terms.

close_reason: Closed

description: a.b.c.d chain-depth check inside method bodies. Pure AST, no graph dependency, no resolver. Standalone analyzer or ast-grep rule. Ship anytime.

description: Refactor existing metrics (cycles/god-module/instability) to filter by edge kind. New gates: feature-envy (own vs foreign call ratio, confidence-guarded), forbidden-USE contracts (import U calls -> 'domain must not use infra'), composition cycles (holds), interface/LSP (inherits). NOTE: dead-import is NOT a graph gate - ruff F401 + vulture own it soundly. Generalize import-linter forbidden/layers to edge-kind-aware; retire import-linter once matched.

title: A3 Batch2: call resolver (annotation/declared-type attribution)

Fuel = A2 holds edges. Return-chain extension; reflection escape-hatch (# arch: ignore). calls subset of import under our constraints -> sound -> hard-gateable.

title: arch-viz: archmap extension (edge kinds, filtering, changelog diff)
description: Extend the archmap cytoscape viewer for the typed class graph: per-kind edge coloring+line-style, filtering (kind/role/level + sound-only), method drill-down, and the architecture changelog/diff view surfaced on PRs. See docs/typed_class_graph_design.md.

title: wire pyrefly strict type gate into template (Correctness R1 type-grade leg)
close_reason: Closed

title: shape_contracts: enforce shapes on ALL functions (drop public/class-only scoping)
description: shape_contracts.py only flags PUBLIC methods INSIDE classes: _analyze walks ast.ClassDef bodies only, and _public filters out names starting with '_'. Consequence: private helpers AND all module-level functions (even public ones) escape the shape contract — a bare torch.Tensor/np.ndarray param/return on them is never flagged. Owner decision: shapes must be enforced EVERYWHERE, public/private is unrelated to shape-checking. Fix (contained to this one gate — the _public filter exists nowhere else in devtools; the ClassDef-only walk is shared by graph/lcom/state_candidates but those are legitimately class-analysis): (1) _analyze also walks top-level ast.FunctionDef, not just ClassDef bodies; (2) drop the _public name filter so private funcs are scanned too. Only array/tensor slots are ever flagged, so scalar params are untouched — bounded backlog. Rollout: enforce-then-clean (owner preference over advisory ratchet) => land the gate widening + fix the surfaced backlog in ONE PR per consuming repo so CI never goes red mid-flight. Surfaces e.g. cardiac-seg core/data/dynamic/synth.py _uniform/_smooth_field/_class_map/_gauss_blur/_rician/_kspace_psf + _Paint methods (all currently unshaped). Origin discussion: cardiac-seg PR #45 review.
close_reason: Shipped v1.14.0: shape_contracts now enforces jaxtyping shapes on ALL functions — class methods (public+private) plus module-level funcs — via _functions/_analyze rewrite, dropping _public + ClassDef-only scoping. Only array slots flag (bounded backlog); add_args/run stay exempt. PR #13, tag v1.14.0. Per-repo backlog cleanup (cardiac etc.) lands in each consuming repo's own PR.

title: Add pre-push hook: fast unit tests (shift-left the CI test gate)
description: The template .pre-commit-config.yaml binds only the FAST static gates (ruff/vulture/import-linter/ast-grep/jscpd/arch-fitness/shape-contracts) to the commit event; tests run only in CI. So a change that lints clean but breaks a test CONTRACT (e.g. a public method signature change that a mirror test still calls the old way) passes every local hook and is only caught by CI after push — a wasted round-trip. Real case: mindscape #65 changed Profile.profile(str)->Profile.profile(cls); the mirror test_profile.py still passed a string, lint was green, CI 'tests' went red.
design: Add a pre-push-STAGE hook (pre-commit framework supports 'stages: [pre-push]') running the fast unit suite: 'uv run pytest tests/unit -q'. Rationale for placement: (1) pre-push not pre-commit — pushes are rare vs commits, and ~10-15s of torch-import + unit tests every commit kills flow; on push it's affordable. (2) tests/unit only — no integration/e2e/GPU/download, keep it fast + deterministic. (3) NOT coverage — the coverage floor stays a CI job (slower); this hook only catches breakage, not coverage regressions. Consumers enable with 'pre-commit install --hook-type pre-push' (document in README + the mindscape CLAUDE.md scaffolding note). Ship as a new hook block in the template's .pre-commit-config.yaml; lands in consumer repos via 'copier update'. Parameterize the test path if lint_paths-style config warrants, else default tests/unit.
acceptance_criteria: pre-push hook present in template .pre-commit-config.yaml running 'uv run pytest tests/unit -q' at the pre-push stage; a deliberately-broken unit contract is BLOCKED at push after 'pre-commit install --hook-type pre-push', and passes once fixed; README documents the --hook-type pre-push install step; copier update pulls it into a consumer repo cleanly.
close_reason: Shipped v1.12.0: pre-push unit-tests hook (tests/unit at pre-push stage), README install note, e2e wiring + functional block test. PR #8 merged, tagged.

Theme darkened to near-black (#05060a canvas, #0a0c11 bar). Owner-approved via preview. Ships in viewer.js + index.html.tmpl -> v1.10.0.

Fix (owner): LEFT-click = drill/fold only; RIGHT-click (cxttap) any node = focus/unfocus its aggregated neighbourhood; suppress browser context menu; drop leaf single-click focus (one gesture). title: Don't vendor devtools engine tests into consumers — move to scaffold-level (no opt-in)
description:
OWNER DECISION (2026-07-15): v1.1.0 (full self-gating, commit 117d867) ships the devtools engine unit tests to every consumer via template/tests/unit/devtools/ (11 files). Consumers do NOT need these copied — there are 0 cases where a consuming project should carry the engines' own tests. They are pure AST-fixture logic tests of template/devtools/*.py (e.g. 'from devtools.lcom import Lcom'), not tests of consumer code, and coverage source is ['core','surfscan'] downstream so they add nothing but noise + churn on copier update.

DECISION: remove entirely, NOT behind a copier flag. Move the engine tests OUT of template/ to a scaffold-root test tree (e.g. tests/unit/devtools/) that imports template/devtools (add template/ to sys.path via a root conftest), so the scaffold's own CI gates the engines and consumers never receive them. Reverses the v1.1.0 self-gating choice for the TESTS only (engines still vendored — they run on consumer code; their tests do not).

Touch points found: template/tests/unit/devtools/ (delete after move); copier.yml:15-16 (the not-enable_ml exclusion of test_shape_contracts.py becomes moot); pyproject.toml:12 stale comment references a non-existent tests/unit/test_devtools.py; tests/e2e/test_dogfood.py + test_e2e.py:217 reference the template mirror tests (adjust assertions); sdlc_test/ dogfood render will lose them (expected). Bump minor + copier update consumers (mirage on v1.1.0 currently carries the vendored copy).

Reported by mirage consumer; scaffold maintainer to implement.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by epic p99 (packaging). Owner pivoted: instead of moving engine tests to scaffold-root (still vendoring the .py + churn), extract devtools to an installable git-dep package pinned by tag — kills the source churn entirely AND keeps test-mirror (runs from the package root). d9x's move-tests-out-of-template is subsumed by p99.1 (tests go into the package).

title: EPIC: dogfood the guardrails — hold the scaffold's own devtools engines to the bar they enforce
description:
The scaffold's CI (e2e.yml) runs ONLY pytest — it never lints or arch-gates its own template/devtools/*.py. The engines that enforce the house bar have drifted from it: ruff union select on template/devtools/ = 2 real errors today (a PERF401 for-loop->list.extend in lcom.py). The tools should eat their own dogfood.

SCOPE = the SCAFFOLD repo only (where the engines live, are editable, testable, fixable). NEVER add devtools to a GENERATED project's gate scope: the engines are template-owned, so a consumer can't fix a finding without hand-editing regenerated code (forbidden), and bd 0lh deliberately stripped devtools tests from consumers. Gating devtools in a consumer = a dead-end red build.

APPLICABLE gate subset for the engines (they are single-file CLI scripts, not class-structured app code): ruff + magic_literals + jscpd + graph.py cycle/god-module. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED: (a) ast-grep class-shape ('every helper on a class') — the engines are module-level main()+functions BY DESIGN; gating it flags every engine as a legit violation, the same house-gate-vs-mandated-pattern conflict as bd 8ex; (b) test-mirror — devtools are tested as one bundle (tests/unit/test_devtools.py), not a test-per-engine, and 0lh removed devtools tests from consumers. Children stage the applicable subset.

Fixed the drift it caught (2 PERF401). Applicable subset only — ast-grep class-shape + test-mirror excluded (documented, 8ex/0lh); never gated in consumers (template-owned). Added graph.py --no-test-mirror as the general affordance for a test-less tool tree. 95 tests green.

title: ml jaxtyping ruff waiver is incomplete: --ignore F722 must also include F821 (single-axis dim strings)
description:
The ml ruff gate waives F722 (jaxtyping multi-token dim strings like "n ch t" parse as malformed forward-refs) via the CLI '--ignore F722' in template/noxfile.py.jinja + template/.github/workflows/ci.yml (the CLI --select bypasses pyproject [tool.ruff.lint] ignore, so the waiver must be on the CLI). But a SINGLE-axis jaxtyping dim string — Float[np.ndarray, "n"], Int[np.ndarray, "n"], Float[Tensor, "f"], etc. — parses as a valid forward-reference to an undefined name, so it trips F821 (undefined-name), NOT F722. F821 is in the enforced select via the 'F' pyflakes group.

Result: the shape-contracts gate (now blocking for ml, vip.4) REQUIRES every array boundary to carry a jaxtyping shape, but the ruff gate then REJECTS any 1-D shape — the two gates contradict. A 1-D array (labels [n], a HRF [t], a per-source prior [s]) legitimately has one named axis; forcing dtype-only "" to dodge F821 throws away the cross-argument length-equality that named axes exist to document.

This didn't surface earlier because cardiac/synth had ~0 array boundaries (passed trivially). mindscape is the first repo to annotate at scale (72 boundaries) and hit it immediately (~30 F821s on 1-D shapes).

FIX: the ml waiver should ignore F722,F821 together (jaxtyping's standard ruff accommodation) — update the CLI --ignore in template/noxfile.py.jinja + template/.github/workflows/ci.yml. mindscape applies F722,F821 locally as the stopgap (documented, this task referenced).
acceptance_criteria: ml-domain generated noxfile + ci ruff step use --ignore F722,F821 (or equivalent), so single-axis jaxtyping shapes pass the enforced gate
close_reason: FIXED v1.0.2. The ml jaxtyping waiver now ignores F722,F821 (was F722 only) on all three CLI --select sites (ci.yml/noxfile/pre-commit) + the pyproject [tool.ruff.lint] ignore. F821 = a single-axis shape Float[Tensor,'n'] parsing as a valid forward-ref to an undefined name (F722 = multi-token 'b c h w' = invalid forward-ref syntax). Needed because shape_contracts is now blocking (vip.4) and REQUIRES a shape annotation ruff-F821 would otherwise reject on every 1-D boundary. PROVEN: ruff --select F on Float[Tensor,'n'] fires F821 with --ignore F722 alone, clean with --ignore F722,F821; e2e asserts F821 in the ml pyproject ignore + F722,F821 on the enforced CLI. 96 tests green.

title: SLF001 in the union conflicts with the mandated op-namespace pattern — needs a ruff select/ignore local-slot
description:
v0.14 added SLF001 (private-member-access) to the maximal union select. It STRUCTURALLY conflicts with the py-top-level-function ast-grep gate, which mandates every helper live on a class as a (usually static) method. Consequence: a stateless sibling-helper call OR a module-level function registry must reference `Cls._helper` — which SLF001 flags, though it is NOT a cross-boundary reach-in (ruff cannot express 'same module/class'). Any consumer that did the free-func -> static-method migration hits this en masse: mindscape (269 funcs migrated) got 99 SLF001 hits across 25 files on the v0.13->v0.16 update — registries (FNIRS_FEATURE_FNS = {name: DescriptorBank._f_mean, ...}), cross-sibling static calls (Amplitude._time_axis from DescriptorBank), and runner main-helpers.

The two rules are both scaffold-owned, so the scaffold must reconcile them. Options: (a) drop SLF001 from the union; (b) add a repo-level ruff select/ignore LOCAL-SLOT (parallel to the pep8-naming ignore-names and vulture-ignore-names FACT slots) so a consumer can exempt SLF001 without hand-editing the template-owned [tool.ruff.lint] block — which the consumer policy forbids ('don't hand-edit template-owned to pass a gate'); (c) narrow SLF001's intent to genuine cross-class reach-ins (not expressible in ruff today). 

Recommend (b): the set of union rules that conflict with a given repo's structure is a per-repo FACT, exactly like the naming/dead-code vocab slots — so a select-subtract / extra-ignore slot is the consistent mechanism. mindscape currently carves SLF001 via the per-file-ignores LOCAL-SLOT (runner tree + static-op-namespace/registry core files) as a stopgap; a proper slot lets it drop the file-list and just subtract SLF001 with a documented reason.
close_reason: DONE v0.18.0. SLF001 resolved via the SAME mechanism as E501 (4c2): demoted from the enforced union to the advisory --extend-select surface, house-wide. Rationale: SLF001 STRUCTURALLY conflicts with the py-top-level-function ast-grep gate (that gate forces same-module Cls._helper references SLF001 mis-reads as reach-ins; ruff can't express 'same module'; 99 FP on mindscape). Chose the advisory-demote over the recommended select-subtract LOCAL-SLOT (option b) because a per-repo rule slot = the o70 dodge vector the owner rejected; the conflict is universal (every migrated repo hits it), so the fix belongs in the base for all 3, not a slot. mindscape can now DROP its per-file-ignores SLF001 stopgap. e2e asserts SLF001 absent from enforced select.

title: Runner can't yet express an ml/consumer rule superset — 3 gaps from cardiac 44o4 (F722-ignore BUG, engine argv default, ci repo-step slots)
description:
Found during cardiac-seg's v0.15 adoption (bd cardiac-seg-44o4). The generated runner can't carry a consumer's rule superset without loss. 3 gaps, #1 is a BUG affecting every fresh ml generation:

## GAP 1 (BUG): enforced-lint CLI lacks --ignore F722 for domain=ml
The rendered ci.yml enforced-lint is 'uvx ruff check <lint_paths> --select F,B,...'. An explicit --select BYPASSES pyproject [tool.ruff.lint] ignore, so F722 (in the F family) FIRES on jaxtyping shape strings (Float[Tensor bchw]) — which the template itself ships for domain=ml. Measured on cardiac: 164 F722 errors under the template's exact enforced-lint line. So a FRESH ml repo that writes any jaxtyping annotation gets a red CI from its own generated config. FIX: the enforced-lint CLI adds --ignore F722 when enable_ml (mirror pyproject's ignore on the CLI), OR drive lint from pyproject config instead of an explicit --select. Verify: an ml gen with a jaxtyping boundary passes its own enforced lint.

## GAP 2 (papercut): base engine argv defaults to 'src', not the packages list
devtools/*.py (graph, state_candidates, lcom, data_clumps, magic_literals, shape_contracts) default their positional packages arg to ['src']. The rendered runner passes packages explicitly so it is fine — but any hand-written/no-arg invocation scans a nonexistent 'src' and silently checks nothing (e.g. shape_contracts --assert with no packages = 0 boundaries = vacuous PASS). cardiac hit this converging engines while keeping its own ci (had to add 'core cardioseg' to every call). FIX: render each engine's argparse default to the copier packages list, or make no-arg a hard error not a silent 'src'.

## GAP 3 (design): no ci repo-step LOCAL-SLOTs for repo-specific gates
The template ci has no slot for consumer-specific steps, so a repo whose CI is a superset of the base can't adopt the runner without losing steps. cardiac's superset the template ci can't express: (a) data-skip env var NAME — cardiac reads CARDIAC_DATA, the template renders CARDIOSEG_DATA (project-name-derived) so tests would error not skip; the env var name is a per-repo FACT, needs a slot/answer; (b) pytest --ignore=<viewer tests> + a SEPARATE viewer coverage step (keeps the floor calibrated to library logic); (c) an extra advisory step (PLR2004 un-silenced); (d) shape_contracts --assert BLOCKING while base ships it advisory (per-repo graduation — cardiac is boundary-clean so it blocks; base graduates all-ml-clean later). FIX: ci repo-step LOCAL-SLOT region(s) + a data-env-var answer, so a consumer's superset rides in slots (mechanism identical, contents per-repo) instead of forcing a fork or a loss.

Until these land, cardiac keeps its own ci.yml (rule superset preserved) running the base engines via explicit 'core cardioseg' calls; 44o4 (full runner adoption) completes when the template can express the superset. Origin: cardiac-seg-44o4 + the v0.15 adoption pass.
close_reason: Fixed all 3 gaps in the template (v0.17.0). GAP1 (BUG): enforced-lint CLI now passes --ignore F722 when enable_ml in ci.yml/noxfile/pre-commit — an explicit --select bypasses pyproject ignore, so a fresh ml gen with a jaxtyping annotation no longer red-CIs on its own config. Proven: ruff --select F fires 2xF722 on Float[Tensor,'b c h w'] dim strings, --ignore F722 clears them. GAP2: every devtools engine (graph/shape_contracts/data_clumps/lcom/magic_literals/state_candidates) is now nargs='+' — a no-arg call is an argparse error not a silent 'src' scan (which made shape_contracts --assert vacuously PASS). GAP3: added data_env_var (when:false, project-derived default, overridable — the ml data-skip env NAME is a per-repo FACT) + two ci LOCAL-SLOT regions (ci-lint-steps/ci-test-steps) so a consumer CI superset rides on slots not a fork. Tests: 92 passed. Unblocks cardiac-seg-44o4 (full runner adoption — the template can now express cardiac's ci superset without loss).

description: Gap from cardiac-seg adoption (xwji audit). Parent: vip epic. vip.3 fixed ruff+jscpd = packages (core cardioseg), 'conform don't fork'.
