title: Golden-fixture test convention: shared-builder generator + committed regen entrypoint + oracle pairing
description: First CHECK whether the scaffold already ships any guidance on golden/characterization tests (grep found none obvious). If absent, add a documented convention (template docs + optional helper) so consuming repos do it uniformly. The rules, from the cardiac-seg PR #45 discussion: (1) golden fixtures are DATA (JSON/npz under tests/.../fixtures/), regenerated by a COMMITTED entrypoint (a  or ) that SHARES the test's input builders (no dup) and CALLS THE REAL code under test (never a reimplementation) — deleting the generator (as done in cardiac yxg2) violates the 'one-off run -> committed command' rule. (2) State the SEMANTICS explicitly in the convention: a golden is a CHARACTERIZATION/regression test — it proves behavior did not CHANGE (bit-fidelity across the whole composed pipeline, incl RNG draw ORDER), NOT that behavior is CORRECT. Frozen-bug risk is real. (3) So the convention pairs golden with INDEPENDENT correctness oracles (property asserts: z-score=>std~1, labels in-range, finite; physics/first-principles unit asserts on transforms) so the frozen state is a VALIDATED state, not just 'whatever the code emitted'. (4) Note the equivalence: a full-chain fixed-seed EXACT-VALUE e2e test IS a golden; the fixture form is just packaging for outputs too big to inline. Deliverable = a scaffold doc section (+ maybe a tiny pytest --update-golden helper) capturing this so all repos share one workflow. Origin: cardiac-seg PR #45 review, yxg2 golden.
close_reason: Owner declined goldens as a pattern (cardiac-seg PR#45 — removed the one golden). No golden-fixture convention wanted; prefer value-bearing unit/integration/e2e tests in the tier hierarchy. drn (shape-everywhere) is unaffected/kept.

title: Document the classmethod migration path for op-namespace repos hitting SLF001 (v1.12)
description: v1.12 graduated SLF001 into the enforced ruff union (8ex reopened). But the scaffold ALSO mandates the op-namespace pattern via the ast-grep py-top-level-function rule: every helper must live on its owning class as a staticmethod (no free top-level defs). That pattern STRUCTURALLY produces SLF001 — a staticmethod (or a module main()) calling a sibling private staticmethod Cls._helper() reads as private-member-access. In mindscape this fired 102 SLF001 across core+neuroscan on the v1.10->v1.12 update, all the mandated pattern, none real encapsulation breaks. The two rules contradict: py-top-level-function forces the exact access SLF001 forbids. Current workaround = each adopting repo carves SLF001 back out tree-wide in the per-file-ignores LOCAL-SLOT, which defeats the graduation for op-namespace repos. Options for the scaffold: (a) don't graduate SLF001 while py-top-level-function is enforced (revert to advisory, the v1.0 8ex resolution); (b) ship the tree-wide SLF001 carve as a template default for repos that enable the op-namespace rule; (c) narrow SLF001 to CROSS-class access only (same-class Cls._x is the mandated idiom, OtherCls._y is the real smell) — but ruff can't express that distinction. Recommend (a) or (b): the mandate and the rule can't both be enforced as-is. Found via mindscape copier update to v1.12 (carved locally, documented as the 8ex conflict).
design: RESOLUTION (owner note in copier.yml, 2026-07-16): the premise 'py-top-level-function MANDATES Cls._helper access, conflicting with SLF001' is REFUTED. The two rules are independent axes — py-top-level-function only requires 'a helper lives on a class', NOT static-vs-instance, NOT private naming, NOT class-name calls. SLF001 is a real reach-in signal and STAYS enforced. The wall of hits an op-namespace repo sees on v1.12 comes from ONE self-imposed style: stateless @staticmethod + sibling calls written Cls._helper (a staticmethod has no self, so same-class calls must name the class -> ruff, which does no class-scope analysis, flags them as reach-ins). NOT a rule bug. FIX = @classmethod + cls._helper: cls is allowlisted so same-class calls go clean, the class stays STATELESS (no instance/state), and EXTERNAL callers do NOT change (a classmethod is still called Cls.method(x)). Proven on mindscape (102 hits / 30 files converted this way; genuine cross-module Other._private reaches stay flagged and get promoted to public API — the rule doing its job). ACTION for the scaffold: add this classmethod migration recipe to the adopter docs / SPEC so the next op-namespace repo updating past v1.12 knows the compliant pattern instead of reaching for a tree-wide SLF001 carve.
notes: VALIDATED end-to-end on mindscape (PR #66): 102 SLF001 across ~30 files -> 0 with NO per-file carve, nox lint green (SLF001 enforced + shape_contracts --assert), pytest 332 passed. The migration recipe that worked, in order: (1) op-namespace @staticmethod -> @classmethod, same-class sibling calls -> cls._x (external callers unchanged); (2) module-level def main() -> @classmethod main(cls) inside its class, guard -> Cls.main(); (3) cross-module-shared privates PROMOTED to public API (the real reach-ins) — and a newly-public array boundary then needs a jaxtyping shape if shape_contracts is on --assert; (4) library-private monkeypatches keep a # noqa: SLF001. Parallelizes well across files (mechanical + ruff self-verify). Recommend shipping this as the adopter-doc migration guide so op-namespace repos updating past v1.12 don't reach for a carve.
close_reason: Shipped docs/UPGRADING.md with the v1.12 SLF001 op-namespace migration recipe (@classmethod + cls._helper), linked from README + SPEC. PR #10 merged. Docs-only, no version bump.

title: devtools naming: unify archmap / archviz / architecture triple
description: One feature, three names: module devtools/archmap.py, vendored assets devtools/archviz/, output dir docs/architecture/. Owner: UNIFY to one word (align module + assets dir + output + docs + CLI). Bigger devtools change (renames the package dir, the wheel-shipped assets, write_viewer/write_json paths, the pre-commit + pages wiring, template .gitignore) -> its OWN release + a consumer copier update. P3, deferred. RESOLVED separately (aqs cheap part, done): stale sdlc-devtools/_site/ gitignore removed; _site CI staging name KEPT (GH Pages convention).
close_reason: Won't-do. The three names are not synonyms — they name three distinct LAYERS of the feature: archmap = compute (module/CLI that maps the import graph), archviz = render (vendored cytoscape viewer assets), architecture = output (the docs/ artifact + Pages path). Unifying to one stem would lose the compute/render/output distinction for a cosmetic gain and cost a breaking rename across the wheel + every consumer's committed graph.json path. Self-descriptive names win. Cheap part (stale _site gitignore) already done.

title: Instability / main-sequence coupling gate in graph.py (I = Ce/(Ce+Ca), distance D)
description:
Near-future. Sharpen the R3 coupling axis with Robert Martin`s package metrics — graph.py already computes the raw afferent/efferent counts, so this is additive.

WHAT graph.py HAS: Ca (afferent = fan-in), Ce (efferent = fan-out), betweenness, on the grimp import graph. Enforces direction (import-linter), acyclicity (no cycles), degree (god-module = Ca AND Ce both high).

WHAT TO ADD:
- Instability I = Ce / (Ce + Ca) per module/package (0 = maximally stable/depended-on, 1 = maximally unstable/depends-on-all). Trivial from the counts already gathered.
- (optional, needs an abstractness signal) Abstractness A = abstract-types / total-types; main-sequence distance D = |A + I - 1| — how far a package sits from the ideal stable<->abstract line (the "V"/stable-dependencies principle: arrows point toward stable abstractions).
- Report: a ranked I (and D if A is computed) table in the graph.py EXPLORER output (drop --assert), beside the existing fan-in/out/bottleneck/chokepoint tables.
- Gate: ADVISORY first (a number to watch), ratchet-to-blocking candidate — e.g. "a module moved further from the main sequence than its recorded baseline" (a per-module D ceiling in [tool.structure], same ratchet shape as file_max / magic-literals). Decide the exact assertion with owner before blocking.

WHY IT`S ITS OWN GATE, not god-module: god-module is a DEGREE check (both counts high). Instability is a RATIO/direction check — it catches a module that is unstable (high Ce) yet widely depended-on (high Ca would lower I, so specifically: depended-on things that are themselves volatile), the stable-dependencies violation a degree threshold misses. Complements, doesn`t duplicate.

OPEN: (a) abstractness signal for A/D — Python has no formal abstract/concrete split; approximate via ABC/Protocol/typing-only modules? or ship I only and defer A/D. (b) module vs package granularity. (c) advisory-forever vs D-ratchet; baseline capture. (d) does this belong in the scaffold superset for all repos or ml-only (it`s domain-neutral -> all repos).

Grounding: README "Coupling, in the standard vocabulary" section; Martin, Agile Software Development (SDP/SAP, the main sequence). Origin: owner insight while rewriting the README pyramid (R3 coupling axis). Relates to the convergence epic vip (a new house-bar gate -> rolls out through the pipe once landed).
close_reason: Done v0.16.0: Martin stability metrics in graph.py. instability(g) I=Ce/(Ce+Ca) (in_degree=Ca, out_degree=Ce; edges are importer->imported); abstractness(mod) A=abstract/total classes via AST (_is_abstract: ABC/Protocol base, metaclass=ABCMeta, or @abstractmethod); main_sequence_distance(g) D=|A+I-1|. Both in the explorer report tables. title: Tier 3: reconcile the divergent ruff ratchet across the 3 repos (ambiguous placement)
description:
Parent: sdlc-scaffold-vip. Tier 3 = the gap that is REAL but has NO 3-repo consensus yet on whether/how it becomes house-bar. Filed so its tracked, NOT dropped and NOT declared permanent-local (per owner: nothing is permanently project-local; everything should flow through the pipe eventually — this is the "not yet decided where" bucket).

THE DIVERGENCE (audited 2026-07-14): the 3 repos ruff select ratcheted in DIFFERENT directions off the scaffolds narrow common floor:
- synthscape: scaffolds EXACT narrow list (no ratchet).
- mindscape: broad FAMILIES — C4, UP, T20, PLR, PLC, RUF, SIM, C90 (advisory target; enforced subset grows). Deliberately REJECTED N (383 hits).
- cardioseg: ~20 SPECIFIC codes — N (+ a [tool.ruff.lint.pep8-naming] ignore-names block), SLF001, PERF401, C408/C420, S603/S607, PTH123, E741/E742/E743, PLR0124, PLR1714, PLW3301, ICN001, E402, RUF005/007/010/012/022/046, PLW0108, E731.

WHY AMBIGUOUS: these are mostly 1/3 (cardioseg-only) or conflicting (mindscape broad-family vs cardioseg specific-code; N is ON in cardioseg, REJECTED in mindscape). By the scaffolds superset rule (majority of 3) NONE clears the bar today except SIM (already promoted in Tier 1). Promoting cardioseg`s taste wholesale = fitting the house bar to one repo — the exact anti-pattern the rule forbids.

THE WORK (a decision pass, not code-first):
1. Per-code triage across the 3: for each divergent code, is it (a) a bug-finder all 3 should run (-> promote to superset, roll out), (b) opinionated style that conflicts (-> pick ONE house answer WITH owner, or leave out), or (c) genuinely project-specific (-> a LOCAL-SLOT mechanism in the template, identical mechanism / different contents — e.g. the pep8-naming ignore-names is per-repo VOCAB but the block itself can be a shared slot).
2. Resolve the broad-family (mindscape) vs specific-code (cardioseg) STYLE clash — the scaffold must pick one expression of the select so generated config is identical. This is the core "generic architecture identical" tension.
3. Candidates likely to graduate as adoption widens (revisit each as a 2nd/3rd repo picks it up): SLF001, PERF401, the RUF safe-fix set, C408/C420. N + pep8-naming: decide the house line (cardioseg yes / mindscape no) — probably a domain=ml ML-idiom slot rather than a blanket N.
4. Output: an updated scaffold ruff_select (single source) + a pep8-naming LOCAL-SLOT mechanism, then the copier-update rollout so all 3 reconverge onto the SAME select (their only difference = LOCAL-SLOT vocab).

This is intentionally the slow tier — start Tier 1+2, widen adoption, come back and let evidence (2nd/3rd-repo uptake) decide each code. Origin: cardiac-seg ruff bd l79o / l5q8; mindscape + synthscape pyproject audits.
notes:
POST-ADOPTION UPDATE (mindscape adopted v0.10 on 2026-07-14, bd mindscape-588n): mindscapes line in the divergence above is now STALE — on adoption it took the scaffolds narrow curated select and thereby DROPPED its broad-family advisory + 3 enforced codes. Rule-loss cross-check vs pre-adoption main quantified the drop:
- ENFORCED lost: S101 (no assert in prod), W (pycodestyle warn), E7 (statement-level), T203 (pprint; T20->T201 keeps print/T201). LIVE IMPACT = ZERO — ruff over core+neuroscan+baselines with select S101,W,E7 is clean today. Theoretical only (a future prod assert would go un-gated).
- ADVISORY lost: the broad families (C4/UP/full E/W/PLR/PLC/S/RUF/C90) that fed the y63 graduate-one-family-per-PR ratchet. 6 live advisory items now HIDDEN from the statistics backlog: UP037 x3 (quoted-annotation), C416, C420, UP035 (deprecated-import). mindscapes whole y63 broad-advisory-then-graduate narrative is now moot under the fixed curated select.
So mindscape is now a 2nd repo on the scaffolds narrow list (with synthscape) — the broad-family expression lost a vote. The Tier-3 decision (this task) should consciously rule on: (a) does S101 belong in the house bar (asserts-in-prod is a real axis, 0-cost to re-add since all 3 are clean)? (b) is the fixed-curated-select model replacing the graduate-ratchet the intended house direction (kills y63s mechanism)? (c) the UP/C4 modernization hints — surface via an advisory-only broad slot, or drop by design (copier.yml already calls UP/C4 opinionated churn)? Owner rule: nothing silently lost — on record here for a conscious call, not accepted by default.
RESCOPED by o70: NOT 'parked pending consensus' anymore. Under UNION, cardiacs N / SLF001 / PERF401 / C408-420 / S603-607 / PTH123 / E741-3 / PLR0124-1714 / ICN001 / E402 ALL enter the union base select; every repo FIXES up (mindscapes N-rejection OVERRULED by union, not negotiated — k8c no-shortcuts). ADD: a [tool.ruff.lint.pep8-naming] ignore-names LOCAL-SLOT (the naming VOCAB = FACT; each repos ML/physics symbols) — does not exist yet, create when N lands. Broad-family (mindscape C4/UP) vs specific-code (cardiac): union the RULES regardless; codes-not-families is a presentation choice. STAGING: bump codes into base one-at-a-time paired with each repos fix (blocking-first, no advisory-select). Owner drives per-code order.
SCAFFOLD-SIDE LANDED (v0.14.0): ruff_select is now the UNION = cardiac's full ratchet, byte-identical base — N, E741-3, PLR0124/1714, PLW3301, RUF005/007/010/012/022/046, C408/420, PERF401, PLW0108, E731, E402, ICN001, S603/607, PTH123, SLF001 (on top of narrow floor + SIM). Added the pep8-naming ignore-names FACT slot + the widened tests per-file-ignores carve-out (SLF001/N8xx/PLR0913). JUDGMENT CALLS ON RECORD: OUT = UP (annotation churn), E701/702 (dense compaction is house style), W (formatter owns it); S101 (no-assert-in-prod) LEFT OUT pending owner's conscious call — a real axis all 3 honor by convention, not added by default. REMAINING (keep open): the per-repo FIX rollout is consumer beads (mindscape's 383 N-hits fixed no-shortcuts; synthscape bump) + the S101 owner decision. Reconvergence 'done' = all 3 generate the identical select via copier update.
S101 RESOLVED (owner call, v0.16.0): IN the union base. Rationale — assert is stripped under python -O so a prod invariant on a bare assert silently vanishes; all 3 repos honor it by convention (zero fix when clean). Added to select + the tests per-file-ignores (tests use asserts). SCAFFOLD-SIDE OF vip.2 IS NOW COMPLETE: union select final (cardiac ratchet + S101), pep8-naming/vulture/per-file FACT slots all shipped. REMAINING = pure consumer rollout: mindscape fixes its ~383 N-hits + any S101/others (blocking-first, no shortcuts); synthscape fixes its delta; both set their magic ceilings. Tracked as consumer beads, not scaffold work.

title: Rust/PyO3 rewrite of the analyzers
description:
LOW PRIORITY BY MEASUREMENT, not by taste. Filed so the idea is recorded with the numbers that argue
against it, rather than being re-proposed from intuition.

WHAT IT WOULD BUY: ~1s. All eight analyzers on cardiac-seg (102 files) total ~1.3s of compute, and 182 unit
tests with full AST walks run in ~5s. Compute is not where the time goes.

WHAT ACTUALLY COSTS: startup. ~0.32s process + ~0.19s import per gate, ~12 gates per lint run, so ~6s of
overhead against ~1.3s of work. A Rust rewrite attacks the 1.3s and leaves the 6s untouched.

AND THE STARTUP WIN DOES NOT REQUIRE RUST: f9y.3 gets most of it in Python by running the gates in one
process. Cheaper to build, no per-platform wheels, no build step.

SO THE ONLY HONEST ARGUMENT FOR RUST is a single fast-starting DISTRIBUTED BINARY — the ruff model — where
the win is startup and distribution, not compute. That is a packaging decision, and it trades against the
scaffold's selling point: every tool is `uvx tool@version`, no build, no platform matrix. Revisit only if
(a) f9y.3 has landed and startup still dominates, or (b) a consumer repo appears where analysis time itself
is measurable — neither is true today.

If it is ever done, port the hot engine only (measure which) behind the same report()/run_assert()
interface, so the Python implementation stays the reference and the two can be differentially tested.

value: e2e suite (tests/e2e) can't run natively on Windows — copier's internal git subprocess dies WinError 193 ('%1 is not a valid Win32 application'), even though git/uvx work standalone. Run it in WSL instead: wsl -e bash -lc 'cd /mnt/d/personal_projects/sdlc-scaffold && uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/e2e/test_e2e.py -q'. WSL is Linux so copier works; full suite ~25s. This is the ONLY way to exercise rendered-project gates (ruff format --check on the rendered noxfile, nox, pre-commit) — Windows jinja2-render + ruff checks miss formatting of the generated files.

SCOPE: this session's job is the scaffold itself (template + sdlc-devtools quality/releases). Consumer repos (cardiac-seg, mindscape, synthscape) EMPLOY the scaffold and notify us of problems — their adoption/copier-update/rollout is THEIR lane, not scaffold work. Do not open PRs or push commits in consumer repos. (owner directive, clf epic)
